hypothesis: Incorporating a differentiable physics-consistency regularization term during training that penalizes the mismatch between the directly predicted forces and the energy-gradient forces (i.e., minimizing ||Fnc(R) + ∇R ÊN(R)||^2 across configurations) will yield more physically coherent NNIPs, reduce out-of-distribution prediction errors (εdirect), improve MD stability on adversarial configurations, and calibrate the Force Delta uncertainty metric for more reliable UQ.
